# physicslly

> physicslly is a theoretical physics blog focused on quantum field theory, general relativity, quantum gravity, holography, gauge theory, topology, cosmology, black holes, and the search for deeper unifying structures in fundamental physics.

## Important Pages

- [Home](https://physicslly.github.io/): Main entry point for the site.
- [Archives](https://physicslly.github.io/archives/): Chronological archive of all posts.
- [Categories](https://physicslly.github.io/categories/): Topic-based navigation.
- [Tags](https://physicslly.github.io/tags/): Tag-based navigation.

## Articles

- [Lattice Gauge Theory and the Wilson Loop: A Non-Perturbative Probe of Confinement in Yang-Mills Theory](https://physicslly.github.io/posts/lattice-gauge-theory-wilson-loop-confinement-yang-mills/): How lattice regularization defines Yang-Mills theory non-perturbatively and how the Wilson loop area law diagnoses quark confinement.
- [The Källén–Lehmann Spectral Representation: How the Full Propagator Encodes the Physical Spectrum](https://physicslly.github.io/posts/kallen-lehmann-spectral-representation-propagator-spectrum/): A derivation of the Källén–Lehmann spectral representation for the exact two-point function, its interpretation in terms of the physical particle spectrum, and the constraints unitarity and causality impose on the spectral density.
- [The Heat Kernel Expansion and Seeley–DeWitt Coefficients: Computing the One-Loop Effective Action](https://physicslly.github.io/posts/heat-kernel-expansion-seeley-dewitt-coefficients-one-loop-effective-action/): A derivation of the Seeley–DeWitt coefficients for a Laplace-type operator, and their use in extracting the one-loop effective action, renormalization, and curvature corrections in quantum field theory.
- [Schwinger-Dyson Equations and the Structure of the Quantum Effective Action](https://physicslly.github.io/posts/schwinger-dyson-equations-structure-quantum-effective-action/): A derivation of the Schwinger-Dyson equations from the functional integral, their relation to the quantum effective action, and the structure of the 1PI hierarchy.
- [Reflection Positivity and the Osterwalder–Schrader Axioms: Reconstructing Unitary QFT from Euclidean Correlators](https://physicslly.github.io/posts/osterwalder-schrader-reflection-positivity-euclidean-qft/): How reflection positivity encodes unitarity in Euclidean quantum field theory, and why the Osterwalder–Schrader reconstruction is the rigorous bridge between Euclidean correlators and a Wightman QFT.
- [Spontaneous Symmetry Breaking, the Higgs Mechanism, and Goldstone's Theorem](https://physicslly.github.io/posts/spontaneous-symmetry-breaking-higgs-mechanism-goldstone-theorem/): A rigorous treatment of spontaneous symmetry breaking in quantum field theory: Goldstone's theorem, the Higgs mechanism, the Englert-Brout-Higgs-Guralnik-Hagen-Kibble mechanism, and the structure of the Standard Model scalar sector.
- [Scattering Amplitudes and the S-Matrix Program: From the Optical Theorem to Modern On-Shell Methods](https://physicslly.github.io/posts/scattering-amplitudes-s-matrix-on-shell-methods/): A rigorous treatment of the S-matrix program: LSZ reduction, the optical theorem and Cutkosky rules, dispersion relations, and modern on-shell methods including BCFW recursion and color-kinematics duality.
- [Anomalies, Topology, and Index Theory in Quantum Field Theory](https://physicslly.github.io/posts/anomalies-topology-index-theory-quantum-field-theory/): A rigorous treatment of chiral anomalies and the Atiyah-Singer index theorem, with emphasis on anomaly cancellation conditions, topological invariants, and the structural constraints these impose on consistent quantum field theories.
- [Causal Structure, Penrose Diagrams, and the Singularity Theorems of Hawking and Penrose](https://physicslly.github.io/posts/causal-structure-singularity-theorems/): A rigorous geometric treatment of causal structure in general relativity: conformal compactification, Penrose diagrams, global hyperbolicity, the Raychaudhuri equation, the focusing theorem, and the Penrose-Hawking singularity theorems.
- [String Theory and the Worldsheet: Conformal Gauge and the Graviton](https://physicslly.github.io/posts/string-theory-worldsheet-conformal-gauge/): A rigorous treatment of the worldsheet formulation of string theory.
- [Noether Currents, Ward Identities, and Symmetry Constraints in Quantum Field Theory](https://physicslly.github.io/posts/noether-currents-ward-identities-qft/): A rigorous treatment of Noether currents, Ward identities, and their role as symmetry constraints in quantum field theory: classical and quantum conservation laws, Ward-Takahashi and Slavnov-Taylor identities, anomaly matching, and applications to non-perturbative physics.
- [Entanglement Entropy in Quantum Field Theory: From the Area Law to Holography](https://physicslly.github.io/posts/entanglement-entropy-qft-holography/): A rigorous treatment of entanglement entropy in quantum field theory: reduced density matrices, replica trick, area law, entanglement entropy in conformal field theory, the Ryu-Takayanagi formula, and the role of entanglement in quantum gravity.
- [BRST Symmetry and Cohomological Quantization of Gauge Theories](https://physicslly.github.io/posts/brst-symmetry-gauge-theories/): A detailed exposition of BRST symmetry, its cohomological structure, and applications to perturbative and non‑perturbative gauge‑theory quantization.
- [AdS/CFT Correspondence: Holographic Duality and its Role in Quantum Gravity](https://physicslly.github.io/posts/adscft-holographic-duality-and-quantum-gravity/): An in-depth exploration of the AdS/CFT correspondence, its derivations, implications for quantum gravity, and connections to black hole thermodynamics and operator algebras.
- [Supersymmetry: From Algebra to Phenomenology and Unification](https://physicslly.github.io/posts/supersymmetry-algebra-phenomenology-unification/): A rigorous treatment of supersymmetry: the graded Lie algebra, supermultiplets, superspace and superfields, the Wess-Zumino model, softly broken supersymmetry, the Minimal Supersymmetric Standard Model, gauge coupling unification, and the role of SUSY in string theory and the search for a Theory of Everything.
- [Conformal Field Theory and the Bootstrap Program](https://physicslly.github.io/posts/conformal-field-theory-bootstrap-program/): A rigorous treatment of conformal field theory: the conformal group, primary operators, operator product expansion, radial quantization, conformal bootstrap, minimal models, and the space of conformal theories.
- [Black Hole Thermodynamics and the Information Paradox](https://physicslly.github.io/posts/black-hole-thermodynamics-information-paradox/): A rigorous treatment of black hole thermodynamics: the four laws, Hawking radiation via Bogoliubov transformation, Bekenstein-Hawking entropy, the information paradox, Page curve, replica wormholes, and implications for a theory of quantum gravity.
- [Topology, Geometry, and the Search for Deeper Physical Laws](https://physicslly.github.io/posts/topology-geometry-deeper-physical-laws/): A rigorous treatment of topology and geometry in physics: fiber bundles, characteristic classes, Berry phase and Chern numbers, instantons, anomalies, index theorems, and the role of global structure in unification.
- [Cosmology, Inflation, and the Early Universe](https://physicslly.github.io/posts/cosmology-inflation-early-universe/): A rigorous treatment of modern cosmology: FRW geometry, Friedmann equations, inflation as an effective theory, cosmological perturbation theory, the cosmic microwave background, and the connections between early-universe physics and fundamental theory.
- [Quantum Gravity: The Clash Between General Relativity and Quantum Theory](https://physicslly.github.io/posts/quantum-gravity-clash-general-relativity-quantum-theory/): A rigorous analysis of the fundamental obstacles to quantizing gravity: perturbative non-renormalizability, background independence, the problem of time, black hole thermodynamics, and the conceptual foundations of candidate approaches.
- [Effective Field Theory and Why Fundamental Physics Is Layered](https://physicslly.github.io/posts/effective-field-theory-layered-fundamental-physics/): A rigorous treatment of effective field theory: Wilsonian decoupling, operator expansions, power counting, matching, symmetry constraints, naturalness, and the layered structure of physical law.
- [Renormalization Group Flow and the Meaning of Scale](https://physicslly.github.io/posts/renormalization-group-flow-meaning-scale/): A rigorous treatment of renormalization group flow: Wilsonian RG, beta functions, fixed points, scaling dimensions, the Callan-Symanzik equation, asymptotic freedom, and the meaning of scale in quantum field theory.
- [Gauge Symmetry and the Structure of Fundamental Interactions](https://physicslly.github.io/posts/gauge-symmetry-structure-fundamental-interactions/): A rigorous treatment of gauge symmetry: local invariance, covariant derivatives, non-Abelian field strength, Yang-Mills action, BRST quantization, instantons, anomalies, and spontaneous symmetry breaking.
- [General Relativity and the Geometry of Spacetime](https://physicslly.github.io/posts/general-relativity-geometry-spacetime/): A rigorous geometric treatment of general relativity: metric connections, curvature tensors, geodesics, Einstein field equations from variational principles, the initial value problem, and the limits of classical gravity.
- [Quantum Field Theory as a Framework for Particles and Fields](https://physicslly.github.io/posts/quantum-field-theory-framework-particles-fields/): A rigorous treatment of quantum field theory: canonical and path integral quantization, correlation functions, perturbative and non-perturbative methods, renormalization, and the limits of the framework.
- [The Action Principle as the Language of Fundamental Physics](https://physicslly.github.io/posts/action-principle-language-fundamental-physics/): A rigorous treatment of the action principle: variational calculus, boundary terms, Noether's theorems, constrained systems, and the route to quantization.
- [Why a Theory of Everything Is Difficult](https://physicslly.github.io/posts/why-a-theory-of-everything-is-difficult/): A rigorous analysis of the conceptual and mathematical obstructions to unifying quantum field theory, general relativity, and cosmology.

## Sitemap

- [XML Sitemap](https://physicslly.github.io/sitemap.xml)
